name: Idris 2 Quantitative Type Theory IR
summary: Idris 2's IR carries multiplicities from {0, 1, ω} on every binder. 0-quantity values are erased before runtime; 1-quantity values must be used exactly once; ω-quantity values are unrestricted. One multiplicity dimension subsumes both Granule's grades and traditional erasure markers.

sections.title: Overview
sections.content: Representation layout: every binder in Idris 2 carries a multiplicity from {0, 1, ω}. 0-quantity values are erased before runtime; 1-quantity values must be used exactly once; ω-quantity values are unrestricted. Both type-level data and erased proof witnesses ride as 0; runtime values are 1 or ω.

sections.title: One dimension subsumes linearity + erasure
sections.content:
The IR-design contribution is that one multiplicity dimension subsumes both Granule's grades and traditional erasure markers, keeping the IR simpler than carrying them as separate annotations.

QTT-based languages (Idris 2 is the canonical example) get linearity, runtime erasure, and proof-vs-execution separation from the same machinery. Type-theory background (QTT, McBride 2016) and memory/resource framing live in the type-system and memory-discipline surveys; this record covers the IR-shape consequence.
